div.head:
No. 36.
Earl Granville to Mr. West.
div.p: Sir: The United States minister called upon me this afternoon by appointment. I took the opportunity of handing to him my reply to his note of the 27th instant,See No. 33. relative to the case of William Lane, at the conclusion of which an answer is also promised to the representations of the United States Government with regard to the American citizens detained in Ireland under the protection of person and property act.
Mr. Lowell said that this latter question was one which was causing considerable excitement in the United States.
